original_hypothesis.title: Timestamp Underflow in Dirty Flag Path Permanently Locks NFTs in Contract
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: When a landlord's plot count decreases (by unlocking staked value), _farmPlots uses plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated as the reward timestamp. If lastUpdated is older than toilerState[tokenId].lastToilDate (renter farmed more recently than the landlord's last metadata update), the subtraction (timestamp - _toiler.lastToilDate) underflows in Solidity 0.8.25, reverting the transaction. Since unstakeMunchable, transferToUnoccupiedPlot, and farmPlots all trigger forceFarmPlots, the renter's NFT becomes permanently locked with no recovery path.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The arithmetic underflow is deterministic in Solidity 0.8.25 checked math. The forceFarmPlots modifier is applied to ALL user-facing functions that could move the NFT, creating an inescapable dead-end state. No emergency withdrawal exists.

proof_of_signal.pattern: timestamp = plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated could be < _toiler.lastToilDate; then line 253 (timestamp - _toiler.lastToilDate) underflows and reverts

preconditions: Renter has staked NFT on a high-numbered plotId
Renter has farmed at least once since the landlord's last metadata update
Landlord reduces locked value (unlocks tokens) such that plotId >= new _getNumPlots

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: schnibblesTotal = (timestamp - _toiler.lastToilDate) * BASE_SCHNIBBLE_RATE where timestamp can be < lastToilDate
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Landlord (maliciously or innocently) withdraws locked value. If their metadata wasn't updated after the renter's last farm, timestamp < lastToilDate causes arithmetic underflow.
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Not a profit attack but permanent NFT lock. Valuable Munchable NFTs become irretrievable. A malicious landlord could use this to trap and ransom high-value NFTs.

economic_questions: Under what conditions does plotMetadata.lastUpdated lag behind lastToilDate?
Can a landlord force this condition by unlocking value without triggering updatePlotMetadata?
How many NFTs could be simultaneously affected?
validation_requirements: Confirm that unlocking from LockManager does NOT call updatePlotMetadata on LandManager
Test the full scenario: stake → farm → landlord unlocks → attempt unstake → confirm revert
Verify there is no alternative unstake path or admin recovery function

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Landlord needs locked value to attract stakers, then unlocks it
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Ransom potential equals value of trapped NFTs
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Depends on NFT value; could be 100%+ if high-value NFTs trapped
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Multi-block: attract stakers → wait for them to farm → unlock value

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: None beyond initial locked value (which is recovered on unlock)
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: After renters have farmed but before landlord metadata is updated
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Individual NFT holders suffer total loss of staked NFT value

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All renters whose plotId >= new numPlots and who farmed after landlord's last metadata update
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All NFTs staked on a landlord's higher-numbered plots

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Check LockManager→LandManager callback paths for metadata updates on unlock
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Confirm arithmetic underflow for timestamp < lastToilDate in Solidity 0.8.25

validation_result.mechanism: timestamp underflow in dirty flag path causing permanent NFT lock
validation_result.impact_summary: When a landlord's plot count decreases below a renter's plotId and the landlord's plotMetadata.lastUpdated is earlier than the renter's lastToilDate, an arithmetic underflow in _farmPlots reverts all user-facing functions (unstake, transfer, farm), permanently locking the renter's NFT in the contract with no recovery path.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The _farmPlots function handles the case where a landlord's available plots decrease by setting timestamp = plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated and marking the token dirty. However, if the renter has farmed more recently than the landlord's last metadata update, lastUpdated < lastToilDate. The subsequent calculation `(timestamp - _toiler.lastToilDate) * BASE_SCHNIBBLE_RATE` underflows in Solidity 0.8.25's checked arithmetic, causing a revert. Critically, the dirty flag assignment (which would cause future iterations to `continue` past this token) is in the same transaction and gets rolled back on revert. Since unstakeMunchable, transferToUnoccupiedPlot, and farmPlots all invoke _farmPlots via the forceFarmPlots modifier, every path that could move or release the NFT reverts. There is no emergency withdrawal function in the contract.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The vulnerable code path is clearly present in LandManager.sol. The dirty flag path sets `timestamp = plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated` which can be older than `_toiler.lastToilDate` if the renter farmed after the landlord's last metadata update. The subtraction `(timestamp - _toiler.lastToilDate)` then underflows. The `forceFarmPlots` modifier on all exit functions creates an inescapable deadlock. The `toilerState[tokenId].dirty = true` assignment that would prevent future reverts is rolled back because it's in the same reverting transaction.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: When plots decrease, timestamp is set to plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated. If this value is less than _toiler.lastToilDate (renter farmed after landlord's last metadata update), the subtraction underflows in Solidity 0.8.25 checked math, causing a revert. The dirty=true assignment is rolled back with the revert, so subsequent calls hit the same underflow.

The forceFarmPlots modifier calls _farmPlots before the unstake logic runs. If _farmPlots reverts due to the underflow, the user cannot unstake their NFT. The same applies to transferToUnoccupiedPlot and farmPlots.

calling_sequence: 4. For the affected tokenId, _toiler.dirty is false (never set or rolled back)
8. schnibblesTotal = (T1 - T3) * BASE_SCHNIBBLE_RATE where T1 < T3 → UNDERFLOW → REVERT
9. Entire transaction reverts including dirty=true, NFT remains locked
10. All future attempts hit the same revert
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: User stakes NFT at time T2 on landlord's plotId 5. User farms at time T3 > T2, setting lastToilDate = T3. Landlord's metadata was last updated at T1 < T3 (plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated = T1). Landlord unlocks tokens reducing plot count below 5 (without triggering updatePlotMetadata). User attempts unstakeMunchable → forceFarmPlots → _farmPlots → dirty path triggers → timestamp = T1 → (T1 - T3) underflows → revert. The dirty=true flag is rolled back. Every subsequent call to unstake/transfer/farm hits the same revert path.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: Deployment scripts show standard proxy deployment pattern for LandManager. No special initialization or configuration that would prevent this condition.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The attack is realistic and can occur both maliciously and accidentally. Scenario: (1) Landlord locks value, getting 10 plots. Renter stakes NFT on plotId 7 at time T2. (2) Renter farms at time T3, setting lastToilDate = T3. plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated remains at T1 < T3 (landlord hasn't updated metadata since before renter's farm). (3) Landlord unlocks enough tokens to reduce plots below 7. If the unlock doesn't trigger updatePlotMetadata (which requires AccountManager to explicitly call it), lastUpdated stays at T1. (4) Any call to unstake/transfer/farm reverts due to (T1 - T3) underflow. The renter's NFT is permanently locked. A malicious landlord can deliberately exploit this to trap high-value NFTs.
validation_result.impact_assessment: CRITICAL - Permanent loss of staked NFTs with no recovery mechanism. Any renter whose plotId becomes invalid while plotMetadata.lastUpdated < lastToilDate loses their NFT forever. Multiple NFTs can be affected simultaneously if a single user has multiple staked tokens, as _farmPlots iterates all staked tokens and a single underflow reverts the entire transaction. This also locks ALL other staked tokens for that user, not just the affected one.
recommended_mitigations: Add a safety check before subtraction: if (timestamp <= _toiler.lastToilDate) { toilerState[tokenId].dirty = true; continue; }
Add an emergency unstake function that bypasses _farmPlots (admin-gated or with forfeited rewards)
Fix the off-by-one in the dirty check: change `_getNumPlots(landlord) < _toiler.plotId` to `_getNumPlots(landlord) <= _toiler.plotId`
Ensure that any unlock operation in LockManager triggers updatePlotMetadata via AccountManager to keep lastUpdated current
Consider using min(timestamp, block.timestamp) and max(timestamp, _toiler.lastToilDate) pattern to prevent underflows
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy LandManager with mock dependencies. 2. Set up landlord with locked value for 10 plots, updatePlotMetadata at T1. 3. Renter stakes NFT on plotId 7. 4. Advance time, renter calls farmPlots (lastToilDate = T3 > T1). 5. Reduce landlord's locked value to yield <7 plots (without calling updatePlotMetadata). 6. Renter attempts unstakeMunchable → expect revert due to underflow. 7. Verify NFT is permanently locked by attempting all exit paths.
uncertainties: Whether the LockManager's unlock path triggers AccountManager.updatePlotMetadata — if it always does, the window for this exploit narrows but doesn't close (race condition between farm and unlock)
Whether there are admin functions in inherited BaseBlastManagerUpgradeable that could rescue locked NFTs (not visible in provided code)
The exact frequency of updatePlotMetadata calls in the AccountManager — if every lock/unlock triggers it, the exploit requires more precise timing
additional_testing_needed: Verify the complete LockManager → AccountManager → LandManager.updatePlotMetadata callback chain for all lock/unlock operations
Test whether pausing and unpausing the contract provides any recovery path
Check if there are any proxy upgrade paths that could add emergency withdrawal functionality
Verify the behavior when multiple tokens are staked and only one has the underflow condition (confirms entire user is locked)
